title: An extended ab initio theory of the V$_{\text{B}}^-$ center in hBN: excited states, Jahn-Teller distortion, and pressure dependence
authors: Zsolt Benedek, Ádám Ganyecz, Oscar Bulancea-Lindvall, Gergely Barcza, Viktor Ivády
abstract: Ensembles of negatively charged boron vacancy (V$_{\text{B}}^-$) centers in hexagonal boron nitride (hBN) have emerged as a two-dimensional spin qubit system interfaced with optics to advance nanoscale quantum sensing. However, a comprehensive description of its optically detected magnetic resonance (ODMR) signal remains challenging due to the strongly correlated nature of the excited electronic states involved in its optical cycle. In this work, we model the energetics, structural relaxation, and transition rates of the V$_{\text{B}}^-$ center using a high-level wave-function-based electron correlation method (CASSCF-NEVPT2). We provide a thorough analysis of the excited state fine structure and pseudo Jahn-Teller effects, singlet-triplet quasi-degeneracies, photoluminescence parameters, intersystem crossing pathways, and stress-dependence of the fine structure and decay parameters. Our findings not only clarify the fundamental behavior of the V$_{\text{B}}^-$ center in hBN but also establish the theoretical foundation for advancing the V$_{\text{B}}^-$ center's readout for integrated 2D quantum sensors.
summary: This paper uses advanced ab initio methods to model the electronic and structural properties of the V_B- defect in hBN. It reveals how Jahn-Teller distortions and external pressure influence the spin-dependent optical transitions. These findings are essential for advancing the use of hBN defects in nanoscale quantum sensing.
main_problem: The difficulty in providing a comprehensive description of the optical cycle and ODMR signal in the V_B- center of hBN due to the strongly correlated nature of its excited electronic states.
main_result: The study identifies the optical transitions, characterizes the Jahn-Teller distortions, and quantifies how external pressure and strain affect the zero-field splitting and intersystem crossing rates.
method: High-level wave-function-based electron correlation method using CASSCF-NEVPT2 and quasi-degenerate perturbation theory for spin-orbit coupling.
model_or_system: The negatively charged boron vacancy (V_B-) center in a hexagonal boron nitride (hBN) 2D sheet, modeled using a B60N60H27 cluster.
key_observables: Zero-phonon line (ZPL) energy, radiative transition rates, zero-field splitting (D and E tensors), and intersystem crossing (ISC) rates.
important_parameters: Jahn-Teller stabilization energy (355 meV), transition energy (~2.3 eV), and temperature regimes (static JT below 200 K, dynamic JT above 200 K).
main_assumptions: The study uses a 1D effective phonon model to approximate transition rates and assumes a cluster model to represent the 2D defect environment.
figures_summary: Figure 1 shows the structural representation and orbital visualization of the defect; Figure 2 illustrates the temperature dependence of ZFS; Figure 3 provides a summary diagram of excitation and decay pathways.
paper_structure: The paper begins with the electronic structure and orbital basis, moves to the analysis of excited state fine structure and Jahn-Teller effects, then investigates the impact of temperature on ZFS, and concludes with the effects of external pressure and strain on the optical and spin properties.
why_interesting: It provides a fundamental understanding of the spin-dependent decay pathways and the role of electron-phonon coupling in a 2D defect, which is critical for developing 2D quantum sensors and spin-photon interfaces.
analysis_basis: full PDF text, analyzed in chunks
